steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content: The previous sentence establishes that the statues are "extremely heavy." The sentence with the blank provides specific numerical evidence to confirm this claim.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification/confirmation: sentence 2 proves sentence 1 with a concrete weight.
  • Choice A — "In any case,": signals a concession or summary regardless of prior context. It introduces resignation, not supporting evidence. ✗
  • Choice B — "Earlier,": signals a time shift to a prior moment. The weight is a present fact, not a past event. ✗
  • Choice C — "In fact,": signals confirmation or evidence backing up a prior claim with a stronger or more specific detail. The 5-ton figure proves "extremely heavy." ✓
  • Choice D — "Instead,": signals substitution or contradiction. The weight does not replace or contradict the prior claim. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
